body:
### Your current environment
The output of python collect_env.py ```text Your output of `python collect_env.py` here ```
### Your code version
The commit id or version of vllm ```text vllm v0.24.0 ```
The commit id or version of vllm-omni ```text vllm-omni v0.24.0 ```
### 🐛 Describe the bug ## Summary When serving Qwen3-Omni via `vllm serve --omni` (multi-stage pipeline), the `/metrics` endpoint exposes **two layers** of latency/token metrics: 1. **`vllm_omni:*`** — pipeline-level (orchestrator) 2. **`vllm:*`** — per-stage engine-level (with `{replica, stage}` labels) After processing real requests, **`vllm_omni:*` reports some data** (E2E, tokens, audio), but **all per-stage `vllm:*` time/token metrics remain 0** — despite histogram buckets being registered. By contrast, plain `vllm serve` (single engine, `engine="0"`) produces **complete `vllm:*` metrics** including TTFT, ITL, TPOT, and E2E. This makes it impossible to monitor per-stage TTFT/ITL/TPOT via Prometheus in omni mode, and breaks parity with upstream vLLM observability. --- ## Environment | Item | Value | |------|-------| | Model | `/data/why/Qwen3-Omni-30B-A3B-Instruct` | | vLLM | v0.24.0 | | vllm-omni | v0.24.0 | | Deployment A (broken) | `vllm serve --omni` with 3-stage pipeline (`replica="0", stage="0/1/2"`) | | Deployment B (working baseline) | `vllm serve` single engine (`engine="0"`) | --- ## Reproduction 1. Start omni server: `vllm serve --omni --deploy-config --log-stats` 2. Send 10+ chat completion requests (with audio modality if applicable) 3. `curl http://localhost:/metrics > metrics.log` 4. Compare with metrics from plain `vllm serve` on the same model: `curl ... > vllm_metrics.log` --- ## Observed Behavior (Omni Mode — `metrics.log`) ### `vllm_omni:*` — Partially Working | Metric | Status | Value | |--------|--------|-------| | `vllm_omni:requests_success_total` | ✅ | 10 (`finished_reason="stop"`) | | `vllm_omni:e2e_request_latency_s` | ✅ | count=10, sum=20.89s, avg≈2.09s | | `vllm_omni:prompt_tokens_total` | ✅ | 670 | | `vllm_omni:generation_tokens_total` | ✅ | 1597 | | `vllm_omni:audio_duration_s` | ✅ | count=10, sum=105.93s | | `vllm_omni:audio_rtf` | ✅ | count=10, avg≈0.20 | | `vllm_omni:transfer_*` | ✅ | 10 transfers per hop (0→1, 1→2) | | `vllm_omni:audio_ttfp_s` | ❌ | HELP/TYPE only, no bucket data | ### `vll

comments.author: ZacheryAU
comments.body:
`vllm_omni:audio_ttfp_s`, `vllm_omni:audio_underrun_s`, `vllm_omni:audio_continuity_ok_total` currently are treated as streaming metrics. These data can be collected by explicitly activating streaming mode, like:
```bash
curl http://<ip>:<port>/v1/chat/completions
  -d '{
    "stream": true,
    ...
  }'
  ...
```

title: [Bug]: The time statistics are incorrect when calling the metrics API.
